---
title: "Contact Networks (Python)"
author: "Simon Frost"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
  gfm:
    toc: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
engine: python
---

## Overview

This is the Python companion to the Julia `05_networks` vignette,
exploring contact network types and their effect on epidemic dynamics.

## RandomNet

```{python}
import starsim as ss
import numpy as np
import pylab as pl

sim_random = ss.Sim(
    n_agents=2_000,
    networks=ss.RandomNet(n_contacts=10),
    diseases=ss.SIR(beta=0.05, dur_inf=4, init_prev=0.01),
    dt=1.0, start=0, stop=200, rand_seed=42, verbose=0,
)
sim_random.run()

prev_random = sim_random.results.sir.prevalence.values
print(f"RandomNet peak prevalence: {max(prev_random):.4f}")
```

## MixingPool: frequency-dependent mixing

```{python}
sim_pool = ss.Sim(
    n_agents=2_000,
    networks=ss.MixingPool(n_contacts=10),
    diseases=ss.SIR(beta=0.05, dur_inf=4, init_prev=0.01),
    dt=1.0, start=0, stop=200, rand_seed=42, verbose=0,
)
sim_pool.run()

prev_pool = sim_pool.results.sir.prevalence.values
print(f"MixingPool peak prevalence: {max(prev_pool):.4f}")
```

## MFNet: male–female partnerships

```{python}
sim_mf = ss.Sim(
    n_agents=2_000,
    networks=ss.MFNet(duration=200, participation=ss.bernoulli(p=0.8)),
    diseases=ss.SIR(beta=0.3, dur_inf=20, init_prev=0.05),
    dt=1.0, start=0, stop=365*5, rand_seed=42, verbose=0,
)
sim_mf.run()

prev_mf = sim_mf.results.sir.prevalence.values
print(f"MFNet peak prevalence: {max(prev_mf):.4f}")
```

## Comparing network types

```{python}
pl.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
pl.plot(range(len(prev_random)), prev_random, label="RandomNet", lw=2, color="blue")
pl.plot(range(len(prev_pool)),   prev_pool,   label="MixingPool", lw=2, color="green")
pl.xlabel("Day")
pl.ylabel("Prevalence")
pl.title("SIR Prevalence by Network Type")
pl.legend()
pl.show()
```

## Multiple networks

```{python}
sim_layers = ss.Sim(
    n_agents=3_000,
    networks=[
        ss.RandomNet(name='household', n_contacts=4),
        ss.RandomNet(name='workplace', n_contacts=8),
    ],
    diseases=ss.SIR(beta=0.05, dur_inf=4, init_prev=0.01),
    dt=1.0, start=0, stop=200, rand_seed=42, verbose=0,
)
sim_layers.run()

prev_layers = sim_layers.results.sir.prevalence.values

pl.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
pl.plot(range(len(prev_random)), prev_random, label="Single net (10 contacts)", lw=2, color="blue", ls="--")
pl.plot(range(len(prev_layers)), prev_layers, label="Two layers (4+8 contacts)", lw=2, color="red")
pl.xlabel("Day")
pl.ylabel("Prevalence")
pl.title("Single vs Layered Networks")
pl.legend()
pl.show()
```

## Effect of contact number

```{python}
contacts = [4, 10, 20]

pl.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
for nc in contacts:
    sim = ss.Sim(
        n_agents=2_000, networks=ss.RandomNet(n_contacts=nc),
        diseases=ss.SIR(beta=0.05, dur_inf=4, init_prev=0.01),
        dt=1.0, start=0, stop=200, rand_seed=42, verbose=0,
    )
    sim.run()
    prev = sim.results.sir.prevalence.values
    pl.plot(range(len(prev)), prev, label=f"n_contacts={nc}", lw=2)

pl.xlabel("Day")
pl.ylabel("Prevalence")
pl.title("Effect of Contact Number")
pl.legend()
pl.show()
```
